sectionName: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

questions.passage: Researchers investigated naive CD4+ T cells encountering antigen-presenting cells with altered B7 and PD-L1 expression because T-cell activation requires TCR recognition plus costimulation, while inhibitory checkpoint signals restrain proliferation after antigen exposure. The investigators framed the project around a narrow mechanistic question: whether weak proliferation was caused by absent antigen recognition, missing costimulation, or excessive checkpoint signaling. Rather than treating the phenotype as a single endpoint, they collected biochemical, cellular, and control measurements so that an apparent association could be separated from a direct pathway effect. The primary experiment compared T cells stimulated with peptide-MHC alone, peptide-MHC plus B7, peptide-MHC plus B7 and PD-L1, and the PD-L1 condition with checkpoint blockade. Samples were prepared under matched conditions for cell number, total protein, incubation time, and buffer composition. The main assay used flow cytometry for CD69, IL-2 secretion, and CFSE dilution across three days. Figure 1 reports CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation. Values are means from independent preparations, and the error bars in the original analysis were small enough that the ordering of the groups was reproducible across trials. The authors did not use the figure alone as proof of mechanism; instead, they used it to choose which follow-up experiment would best distinguish between competing explanations. Several controls were built into the design. MHC blocking antibody abolished CD69 induction, viability dye excluded dead cells, and equal peptide loading was confirmed with a conformation-specific antibody. These details matter because many superficially attractive interpretations would predict a global change in viability, loading, or nonspecific assay performance rather than a pathway-specific change. The investigators also measured phosphorylation of proximal TCR signaling proteins, which allowed them to ask whether the perturbation changed expression, localization, catalytic activity, or signal propagation. A follow-up experiment tested whether checkpoint blockade restored proliferation without increasing peptide-MHC abundance. This experiment was deliberately designed to avoid a common interpretive trap: early activation marker expression is not the same as sustained clonal expansion. They avoided claiming that every downstream physiological effect had been proven, because the study did not include whole-animal outcomes or long-term compensation. The figure provides quantitative evidence about the immediate assay readout, the controls determine whether that readout is technically interpretable, and the follow-up experiment tests whether the proposed mechanism survives an additional perturbation.
questions.stem: Which interpretation best fits the CD69 and IL-2/division data?

choices.A: PD-L1 reduces sustained proliferation even though early antigen recognition is preserved, consistent with inhibitory checkpoint signaling.
choices.B: PD-L1 prevents peptide-MHC recognition entirely, because CD69 should be absent whenever PD-L1 is present, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: B7 primarily suppresses IL-2 secretion, because costimulation should make T cells anergic.
choices.D: Checkpoint blockade works by removing peptide from MHC, forcing T cells to proliferate without antigen.

explanations.A: Correct. The data separate early TCR engagement from sustained clonal expansion. The relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. CD69 is an early activation marker, whereas IL-2 and CFSE dilution report a more durable proliferative program. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.B: CD69 remains high in the PD-L1 condition, so antigen recognition and early activation are not abolished. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: B7 increases IL-2/division in the figure, which is the opposite of a suppressive interpretation. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: MHC blockade abolishes CD69, and the follow-up does not propose reducing peptide-MHC abundance. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. Early activation and proliferative responses under costimulatory and inhibitory conditions.
questions.passageHelp:
The researchers concluded that the data supported a specific model, but they noted that the passage evidence was strongest for the step directly measured by the assay.

In interpreting the study, the reader should keep the experimental hierarchy in mind.

Answers that merely name a molecule from the passage are usually weaker than answers that explain how the measured intervention changes the pathway while leaving alternative explanations less consistent with the observations.

A strong answer therefore has to account for the numerical trend, the biological pathway, and the limits of the design.

questions.stem: Which mechanism best explains the effect of B7 in the experiment?

choices.A: B7 replaces the TCR, so peptide-MHC is unnecessary for any early activation marker, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.B: B7 acts as a terminal complement component that lyses antigen-presenting cells.
choices.C: B7 provides costimulatory signaling that increases IL-2 production and cell division after TCR engagement.
choices.D: B7 blocks all cytokine secretion while preserving only surface-marker staining.

explanations.A: MHC blocking antibody abolishes CD69 induction, so TCR recognition remains necessary. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: B7 is a costimulatory ligand in T-cell activation, not a complement pore-forming protein. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Correct. The B7 condition produces much higher IL-2/division than peptide-MHC alone. The relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Costimulation amplifies T-cell activation after antigen specificity has been established by the TCR. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.D: IL-2/division rises strongly in the peptide-MHC plus B7 condition. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: Why is the MHC-blocking antibody control important?

choices.A: It directly measures the number of T-cell receptor genes in each cell.
choices.B: It verifies that CD69 induction depends on peptide-MHC recognition rather than nonspecific stimulation of the T cells.
choices.C: It proves that PD-L1 is absent from every antigen-presenting cell in the experiment, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: It converts CD4+ T cells into B cells so antibody secretion can be measured.

explanations.A: Blocking antibody is a functional perturbation, not a gene-copy assay. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Correct. When MHC is blocked, the antigen-recognition step should fail. The relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. This control localizes early activation to the TCR-peptide-MHC interaction rather than culture stress. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.C: MHC blockade tests antigen presentation, not PD-L1 expression. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: The passage does not describe lineage conversion or B-cell differentiation. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: Which follow-up result would most strongly support checkpoint inhibition as the cause of weak proliferation?

choices.A: Removing B7 produces the same high division as adding B7 plus checkpoint blockade, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.B: Blocking PD-L1 restores IL-2 secretion and CFSE dilution without increasing peptide-MHC loading.
choices.C: Blocking PD-L1 eliminates CD69 in the B7 condition while leaving IL-2 unchanged.
choices.D: Adding excess viability dye increases proliferation in every culture.

explanations.A: Loss of costimulation should not mimic the rescue condition if B7 is needed for IL-2/division. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Correct. A checkpoint model predicts rescue of proliferation downstream of intact antigen recognition. The relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. The key is that rescue should change the inhibitory signal, not the amount of peptide-MHC presented. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.C: That would imply loss of early recognition rather than rescue of checkpoint-limited proliferation. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: A viability dye is a measurement reagent, not a costimulatory or checkpoint intervention. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: Which conclusion would be too broad?

choices.A: B7 increases IL-2/division relative to peptide-MHC alone.
choices.B: MHC blockade helps test whether early activation is antigen dependent, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: High CD69 expression alone proves that the T cells have completed sustained clonal expansion.
choices.D: PD-L1 can reduce division while CD69 remains high.

explanations.A: The figure directly supports that comparison. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: That is the purpose of the blocking antibody control. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Correct. CD69 is an early marker and does not by itself prove multi-day proliferation. The relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. The passage specifically distinguishes early activation from sustained clonal expansion. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.D: That is the central pattern in the PD-L1 condition. Here, the relevant evidence is that CD69 rose with peptide-MHC in all antigen conditions, IL-2 and division increased with B7, and PD-L1 reduced division despite preserved early activation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.passage: Researchers investigated serum samples from patients with recurrent encapsulated bacterial infections because classical, lectin, and alternative complement pathways converge on C3 activation but differ in the initiating recognition event. The investigators framed the project around a narrow mechanistic question: whether the defect was upstream of C3, at C3 itself, or in terminal membrane-attack complex formation. Rather than treating the phenotype as a single endpoint, they collected biochemical, cellular, and control measurements so that an apparent association could be separated from a direct pathway effect. The primary experiment compared normal serum, patient serum, patient serum supplemented with purified C3, and patient serum supplemented with C5-C9 proteins. Samples were prepared under matched conditions for cell number, total protein, incubation time, and buffer composition. The main assay used opsonization of fluorescent bacteria and hemolysis of antibody-coated erythrocytes. Figure 1 reports patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did. Values are means from independent preparations, and the error bars in the original analysis were small enough that the ordering of the groups was reproducible across trials. The authors did not use the figure alone as proof of mechanism; instead, they used it to choose which follow-up experiment would best distinguish between competing explanations. Several controls were built into the design. Total IgG concentration was normal, bacterial input was matched, and heat-inactivated normal serum served as a negative control. These details matter because many superficially attractive interpretations would predict a global change in viability, loading, or nonspecific assay performance rather than a pathway-specific change. The investigators also measured C3 convertase activity after alternative-pathway stimulation, which allowed them to ask whether the perturbation changed expression, localization, catalytic activity, or signal propagation. A follow-up experiment tested whether restoring C3 corrected both opsonization and downstream terminal activity. This experiment was deliberately designed to avoid a common interpretive trap: failure of lysis can result from poor upstream C3 activation even when terminal proteins are present. They avoided claiming that every downstream physiological effect had been proven, because the study did not include whole-animal outcomes or long-term compensation. The figure provides quantitative evidence about the immediate assay readout, the controls determine whether that readout is technically interpretable, and the follow-up experiment tests whether the proposed mechanism survives an additional perturbation.
questions.stem: Which interpretation best fits the supplementation data?

choices.A: The bacteria cannot bind IgG, because total IgG concentration is absent in patient serum.
choices.B: The defect is isolated to C9, because terminal C5-C9 supplementation fully restores C3b deposition.
choices.C: Heat-inactivated normal serum should have the strongest complement function because heat activates all pathways, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: The patient phenotype is most consistent with impaired C3 function or activation rather than an isolated terminal-complement defect.

explanations.A: The passage states total IgG concentration is normal. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: C5-C9 supplementation does not restore C3b deposition well, so an isolated terminal defect is unlikely. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Heat inactivation is a negative control that disrupts complement activity. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. Purified C3 restores opsonization better than terminal components. The relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. C3 is central to opsonization and also feeds downstream terminal pathway activity. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. Bacterial C3b deposition and antibody-coated erythrocyte hemolysis after supplementation.
questions.passageHelp:
The researchers concluded that the data supported a specific model, but they noted that the passage evidence was strongest for the step directly measured by the assay.

In interpreting the study, the reader should keep the experimental hierarchy in mind.

Answers that merely name a molecule from the passage are usually weaker than answers that explain how the measured intervention changes the pathway while leaving alternative explanations less consistent with the observations.

A strong answer therefore has to account for the numerical trend, the biological pathway, and the limits of the design.

questions.stem: Why can poor C3 activation reduce hemolysis even if terminal proteins are present?

choices.A: Terminal proteins synthesize C3 mRNA inside erythrocytes during the hemolysis assay.
choices.B: C3b is the antibody heavy chain that directly recognizes bacterial capsule polysaccharide, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: C3 blocks all membrane attack complexes, so adding C3 should always reduce lysis.
choices.D: C3 activation helps generate downstream convertase activity needed to reach efficient C5 cleavage and MAC formation.

explanations.A: Erythrocytes do not use terminal complement proteins to synthesize C3 mRNA. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: C3b is a complement fragment used for opsonization, not an antibody chain. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: C3 supports complement amplification and downstream activation, and C3 supplementation improves hemolysis here. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. C3 lies upstream of both opsonization and efficient terminal pathway activation. The relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Complement questions often test convergence: defects at a central upstream step can affect multiple endpoints. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.stem: What does normal total IgG help rule out?

choices.A: It argues against low antibody abundance as the primary reason for poor classical-pathway activation.
choices.B: It demonstrates that bacteria were omitted from the opsonization assay.
choices.C: It proves that alternative-pathway amplification is absent in normal serum, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: It shows that patient serum contains no complement inhibitors of any kind.

explanations.A: Correct. The passage reports normal total IgG concentration in patient serum. The relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. A normal antibody level makes the complement defect less likely to be due simply to missing antibody trigger. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.B: Bacterial input was matched separately and is not determined by IgG concentration. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: IgG concentration does not measure alternative-pathway amplification. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Normal IgG does not exclude every possible complement regulator abnormality. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: Which follow-up would best localize the defect to C3 convertase formation?

choices.A: Adding terminal C5-C9 proteins eliminates the need to measure any upstream convertase.
choices.B: Measuring red blood cell glycolysis identifies the complement protein that recognizes capsule polysaccharide, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: Removing bacteria from the assay increases C3b deposition on the bacterial surface.
choices.D: Directly measuring alternative-pathway C3 convertase activity shows reduced activity in patient serum that improves after adding purified C3.

explanations.A: The original data show terminal supplementation does not restore C3b deposition well. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: RBC glycolysis is not a complement-pathway localization assay. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Without bacteria there is no bacterial surface to opsonize. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. Convertase measurement directly tests the step responsible for C3 cleavage and amplification. The relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. A pathway-localizing assay is stronger than another downstream endpoint alone. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.stem: Which conclusion would go beyond the data?

choices.A: The defect is not best explained by absent total IgG.
choices.B: The passage proves the exact inherited mutation in the patient complement gene.
choices.C: Low C3b deposition can reduce bacterial opsonization.
choices.D: C3 supplementation improves opsonization more than terminal-protein supplementation.

explanations.A: Normal IgG makes that interpretation unlikely. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Correct. The assays localize function but do not sequence the patient genome. The relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Functional rescue can suggest a pathway defect without identifying the exact allele. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.C: That is a direct functional role of C3b. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: That is shown in the figure. Here, the relevant evidence is that patient serum had low C3b deposition and low hemolysis; purified C3 restored opsonization better than terminal components did The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
